arcanaSignature.title: Arcana Signature
arcanaSignature.subtitle: A reflective mirror drawn from your birthday
arcanaSignature.description: Arcana Signature is a symbolic reflection built from your date of birth. Using only the Major Arcana, it surfaces two personal archetypes — a Personality Card and a Soul Card — offered as a gentle invitation to notice the themes you may wish to explore. It is a mirror for self-discovery, not a forecast.

positions.name: Personality Card
positions.description: The outward archetype — the energy you tend to meet the world with, and the themes that may colour how others experience you.

positions.name: Soul Card
positions.description: The inward archetype — a quieter underlying current that speaks to what nourishes you and what you may be growing toward.

supportedFocuses.label: Love
supportedFocuses.description: Read your signature through the lens of connection, closeness, and the heart.

supportedFocuses.label: Work
supportedFocuses.description: Read your signature through the lens of vocation, effort, and direction.

supportedFocuses.label: Self-Growth
supportedFocuses.description: Read your signature through the lens of self-awareness, habits, and inner change.

supportedFocuses.label: Healing
supportedFocuses.description: Read your signature through the lens of gentleness, rest, and tending to what needs care.

supportedFocuses.label: Creativity
supportedFocuses.description: Read your signature through the lens of imagination, making, and self-expression.

calculation.description: Your two cards are calculated from your birth date, never drawn at random. The month, the day, and the two halves of the year are added together and gently reduced to a Major Arcana value between 1 and 22 — that value is your Personality Card. Reducing it once more to a single digit gives your Soul Card. The same birthday always points to the same signature.
calculation.threeCardExceptionNote: When the calculation reaches 19 (The Sun), the traditional reduction passes through Wheel of Fortune (10) on its way to The Magician (1). Arcana Signature shows two cards — The Sun and The Magician — and keeps Wheel of Fortune as a quiet connecting thread rather than a third card.

default.opening: This is your Arcana Signature — a symbolic reflection drawn from your birthday and offered as a mirror, not a map.
default.personalityIntro: Your Personality Card is {personalityCardName}, the outward archetype you tend to meet the world with. It gathers around themes of {personalityKeywords}. {personalitySummary}
default.soulIntro: Your Soul Card is {soulCardName}, a quieter inner current beneath the surface. It leans toward {soulKeywords}. {soulSummary}
default.combinedReflection: Held together, {personalityCardName} and {soulCardName} sketch a personal pattern — {pairTheme}. {pairReflection}
default.focusReflection: Seen through the lens of {focusLabel}, notice how {personalityCardName} and {soulCardName} might colour this part of your life. Hold it as an invitation to explore rather than a conclusion.
default.journalPrompt: Where do you sense {personalityCardName} in how you meet your days, and where does {soulCardName} quietly ask for your attention?
default.closing: Let your signature be a gentle prompt for self-discovery. These are themes you may explore — a reflective mirror, never a statement about who you must be.

love.focusReflection: In matters of {focusLabel}, {personalityCardName} suggests how you tend to show up for closeness, while {soulCardName} hints at what your heart quietly needs. Notice where the two meet.
love.journalPrompt: In connection, where does {personalityCardName} lead, and what would it mean to honour {soulCardName} as well?

work.focusReflection: In your {focusLabel}, {personalityCardName} colours how you take action and direction, while {soulCardName} speaks to the deeper motivation underneath. Where might they support each other?
work.journalPrompt: What kind of work would let both {personalityCardName} and {soulCardName} feel expressed?

self_growth.focusReflection: For {focusLabel}, {personalityCardName} shows a familiar outward pattern, while {soulCardName} points toward what is quietly unfolding within. Growth often lives in the conversation between them.
self_growth.journalPrompt: What small, kind step would let {soulCardName} grow alongside {personalityCardName}?

healing.focusReflection: Around {focusLabel}, {personalityCardName} may show how you cope on the surface, while {soulCardName} names what gently needs tending. Let care move at its own pace.
healing.journalPrompt: What would tending to {soulCardName} look like today, without rushing {personalityCardName}?

creativity.focusReflection: In {focusLabel}, {personalityCardName} shapes how your ideas reach the world, while {soulCardName} feeds the wellspring they rise from. Notice what wants to be made.
creativity.journalPrompt: What would you create if {personalityCardName} and {soulCardName} worked together freely?

the_magician__wheel_of_fortune.reflection: This signature brings together the Magician’s gift for intention and the Wheel of Fortune’s reminder that life moves through seasons. You may recognize a pattern of turning uncertainty into movement: gathering what is available, choosing a clear next step, and adapting when the path shifts. The invitation is not to control every change, but to meet each turn with presence, skill, and creative trust.

the_high_priestess__justice.reflection: This signature joins the High Priestess’s quiet intuition with Justice’s call for clarity, fairness, and truth. You may feel drawn to understand what is hidden beneath the surface while also needing choices to feel clean, balanced, and accountable. The invitation is to let inner sensing and clear discernment support one another, so wisdom can be both deeply felt and carefully lived.

the_empress__the_hanged_man.reflection: This signature blends the Empress’s warmth, creativity, and care with the Hanged Man’s willingness to pause, soften, and see differently. You may carry a generous instinct to nurture what is growing, yet also learn through moments when life asks you to wait or surrender control. The invitation is to trust that growth is not always rushed; sometimes it ripens through stillness, tenderness, and a changed point of view.

the_emperor__death.reflection: This signature brings the Emperor’s steadiness, protection, and structure into conversation with Death’s deep process of release and renewal. You may feel the tension between building something lasting and recognizing when an old form has completed its purpose. The invitation is to create foundations that are strong enough to support change, and flexible enough to let life breathe through them.

the_hierophant__temperance.reflection: This signature joins the Hierophant’s respect for tradition, teaching, and belonging with Temperance’s art of blending, moderation, and gentle integration. You may be drawn to meaning, guidance, and shared values, while also needing space to adapt what you have inherited into something livable and kind. The invitation is to become a bridge: honoring wisdom without becoming rigid, and creating harmony without losing depth.

the_lovers__the_devil.reflection: This signature places the Lovers’ theme of meaningful choice beside the Devil’s mirror of attachment, temptation, and unconscious pattern. You may feel life most strongly where love, desire, loyalty, and freedom meet. The invitation is to notice the difference between bonds that nourish you and bonds that narrow you, so connection can become more honest, embodied, and free.

the_chariot__the_tower.reflection: This signature combines the Chariot’s focus, movement, and self-direction with the Tower’s clearing force. You may carry a strong instinct to move forward, yet life may also teach you through moments that rearrange the road beneath your feet. The invitation is to let courage be more than control: to choose your direction when you can, and to respond with presence when change arrives on its own.

strength__the_star.reflection: This signature brings Strength’s gentle bravery together with the Star’s quiet renewal after difficulty. You may carry a kind of resilience that does not need to harden in order to survive. The invitation is to trust softness as a form of power: the patience to stay with yourself, the courage to remain open, and the hope that slowly returns when you make space for healing light.

the_hermit__the_moon.theme: Solitude, mystery, and the inner lantern
the_hermit__the_moon.reflection: This signature joins the Hermit’s patient search for inner truth with the Moon’s realm of dreams, uncertainty, and hidden feeling. You may be naturally drawn to what lies beneath appearances, preferring depth over easy answers. The invitation is to walk gently with mystery, using solitude as a lantern rather than a wall, and allowing unclear feelings to become questions you can meet with compassion.

the_sun__wheel_of_fortune__the_magician.theme: Radiance moving through cycles into action
the_sun__wheel_of_fortune__the_magician.reflection: This signature carries the Sun’s warmth, vitality, and clarity into the Magician’s ability to shape intention into action. Between them, the Wheel of Fortune becomes a quiet bridge: a reminder that growth often moves in cycles, not straight lines. The invitation is to let confidence and skill work together, meeting each season with creativity, perspective, and renewed willingness to begin.
the_sun__wheel_of_fortune__the_magician.bridgeNote: In the traditional reduction, this signature passes through Wheel of Fortune (10) on its way from The Sun (19) to The Magician (1). Arcana Signature keeps Wheel of Fortune as a quiet connecting thread — a reminder of changing seasons — rather than a third visible card.

the_high_priestess__judgement.theme: Private wisdom and the call to awaken
the_high_priestess__judgement.reflection: This signature brings the High Priestess’s inward listening together with Judgement’s call to rise, respond, and live more consciously. You may sense a rhythm between retreat and expression: times when truth needs silence, and times when it asks to be answered. The invitation is to trust the wisdom that gathers quietly within you, then let it guide the moments when you choose to step forward.

the_empress__the_world.reflection: This signature joins the Empress’s nurturing abundance with the World’s sense of completion, belonging, and integration. You may feel most aligned when care becomes creation, and creation becomes connection. The invitation is to notice what you are tending with love, how it belongs to a larger circle, and where receiving, giving, beauty, and completion can meet without force.

the_fool__the_emperor.reflection: This signature brings the Fool’s openness, curiosity, and trust into relationship with the Emperor’s structure, steadiness, and responsibility. You may carry both the impulse to begin freely and the need to give that beginning a form that can last. The invitation is to let wonder and discipline become allies: a free spirit with a safe foundation, and a strong foundation that still leaves room for wonder.

privacyCopy.birthdayHelper: Your birthday is used only to calculate your two Arcana Signature cards. It stays on your device and is never used to make claims about your future.
privacyCopy.focusHelper: Choosing a focus is optional. It only shifts the angle of reflection and the journal prompts — it never changes which two cards your birthday calculates.
privacyCopy.localStorageHelper: Your Arcana Signature is calculated and kept locally on your device. You can clear it at any time.
